hypotheses: The implementation of out_sop and out_pos in TopModule is correct for the 12 required input combinations, but does not match the expected outputs for the don't-care input cases, leading to mismatches during the exhaustive and random input phases of the testbench [evidence: log_excerpt L8 'passes on the 12 required input combinations, but doesn't match the don't-care cases', L12 'Total mismatched samples is 17 out of 107 samples'].

The logic equations for out_sop and out_pos in TopModule may be over-constrained or not fully minimized, causing incorrect outputs for input combinations outside the explicitly listed minterms/maxterms [evidence: RTL L25-L27, L44-L53; log_excerpt L8, L10, L11].

The testbench applies all 16 possible input combinations (and additional randoms), but the TopModule logic only guarantees correct outputs for the 12 required cases, so the remaining 4 (plus randoms) are producing mismatches [evidence: testbench L52-L54, L57-L58; log_excerpt L8, L12].
likely_failure_points: The logic for out_sop in TopModule (lines 25-27) does not account for don't-care input combinations, resulting in 10 mismatches, first at time 170 [evidence: log_excerpt L10, RTL L25-L27].
The logic for out_pos in TopModule (lines 44-53) similarly does not account for don't-care input combinations, resulting in 7 mismatches, first at time 220 [evidence: log_excerpt L11, RTL L44-L53].

The testbench's exhaustive and random input application (lines 52-54, 57-58) exposes the don't-care cases, which the DUT does not handle as expected [evidence: testbench L52-L54, L57-L58; log_excerpt L8, L12].
recommended_probes: Add probes to monitor the values of a, b, c, d, out_sop, and out_pos at every clock cycle, especially for input combinations not explicitly listed in the minterms/maxterms.
Add a probe to compare out_sop_dut and out_sop_ref, and out_pos_dut and out_pos_ref, for all input combinations, and log the input vector when a mismatch occurs.
Add a probe to record which input combinations correspond to the first mismatches at times 170 (out_sop) and 220 (out_pos) to confirm if these are don't-care cases.
analysis_notes: The evidence is strong that the DUT logic is correct for the 12 required input combinations but does not match the reference for the remaining input cases, as indicated by the testbench's hint and the mismatch statistics. The testbench applies all 16 input combinations and additional randoms, so the don't-care cases are being tested and are causing the mismatches. The RTL equations are explicitly written for the required minterms/maxterms, but do not specify behavior for the don't-care cases, which is likely the root cause. No prior attempts or patch strategies exist, so this is a first diagnosis. The evidence is sufficient to support these conclusions.
